evals.prompt: I have a Java Spring Boot monolith at /home/z/my-project/test-codebase/banking-app. It has about 50K lines of code with REST controllers, JPA entities, and a PostgreSQL database. Can you extract the business capabilities from this codebase? Save the output to /home/z/my-project/test-output/.
evals.expected_output: A capability-output directory with a1-candidates.md containing candidates grouped by HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW confidence with evidence trails, a2-analysis.md with confirm/split/merge/de-scope actions, a4-l1-locked.md with finalized L1 capabilities, a5-l2-per-capability.md with L2 sub-capabilities including code locations and entities, and a6-domain-model.md with a consolidated hierarchy.
expectations: The output includes a1-candidates.md with candidates rated by confidence
Candidates include evidence from at least package structure and entry points
The output includes a2-analysis.md with actions (confirm/split/merge/de-scope) for each candidate
The output includes a4-l1-locked.md with a finalized L1 capability list
The output includes a5-l2-per-capability.md with L2 operations mapped to specific files
The output includes a6-domain-model.md with a capability hierarchy tree
No delivery channels, infrastructure layers, or cross-cutting concerns are listed as business capabilities

evals.prompt: We're modernizing a legacy Python Django application. The repo is at /home/z/my-project/test-codebase/insurance-claims. It has no database access available (the DB is behind a VPN we can't reach), and there's no frontend — it's a pure API backend. Can you map out what business capabilities exist in this code?
evals.expected_output: The pipeline skips A1.2 (database) and A1.4 (frontend) as specified in the skill's adaptive behavior. Outputs still include all other phases with a note about skipped steps.
expectations: The pipeline skips A1.2 (database schema analysis) since no DB access is available
The pipeline skips A1.4 (frontend entry point analysis) since there is no frontend
The output includes a1-candidates.md noting which signal sources were available and which were skipped
Candidates are still generated from available signals (package structure and backend entry points)
The output includes a complete a6-domain-model.md despite missing signal sources

evals.prompt: Our CTO wants a quick domain model from our Node.js microservices repo at /home/z/my-project/test-codebase/ecommerce. We have an OpenAPI spec at /home/z/my-project/test-codebase/ecommerce/api-spec/openapi.yaml. Focus on the domain model — we don't need the full industry comparison.
evals.expected_output: The pipeline runs A1–A6, using the OpenAPI spec as a pre-generated input for A1.3. A7 may be brief or skipped based on user preference. The domain model traces capabilities to code locations.
expectations: The pipeline uses the OpenAPI spec as a pre-generated input for entry point analysis
The output includes a6-domain-model.md with the capability hierarchy
L2 sub-capabilities include specific code locations (file paths)
Each capability entry includes key operations derived from the API spec
The output does NOT include a7-benchmark.md or notes that it was deprioritized per user request
